turns.user_message: I used to actually look forward to tackling my daily tasks, but lately I just feel completely drained before I even start. It has been dragging on for months now, and I keep hoping it will just pass on its own. Every single morning feels heavier than the last, and I find myself staring at the same list without making any real progress. I try to push through like I always used to, but my energy just evaporates halfway through the day. I know I should be able to handle this, but it feels like I am running on empty no matter how much I rest.

I remember when finishing a project actually gave me a sense of pride, but now it just leaves me feeling hollow and completely detached. This pattern has been sticking around for so long that I barely recognize my own routine anymore. I keep showing up and going through the motions, yet nothing seems to land or stick in my head the way it used to. The hours just blur together into one long stretch of mental fog that I cannot seem to shake off. I keep telling myself I will bounce back soon, but the weight just keeps piling up day after day.

There was a time when I could easily bounce back from a rough week, but lately I feel completely stuck in a heavy rut that refuses to lift. It has been dragging on for months, and I can barely muster the focus to get through the simplest tasks without my mind wandering off. I keep trying to force myself to care the way I used to, but everything just feels like wading through thick mud. The constant pressure has worn me down to a point where even small requests feel completely overwhelming and impossible to tackle. I just sit there watching the clock tick by, wondering how I lost my drive so completely.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This has been going on for a while.
